issue_title: Users cache clears after creating client scope.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

While updating Keycloak to a distribution based on Quarkus, we encountered the following issue: in a clustered installation consisting of, for example, three nodes, creating a client scope on one node completely clears the user cache for the realm where the client scope was created on the other nodes.

This behavior has been observed in all versions after commit  [7855b933909127937672c5ce60ede1e065b6bb2d](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/7855b933909127937672c5ce60ede1e065b6bb2d)  

### Version

19.0.0 or higher

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The user cache content should not be cleared after creating a client scope.

### Actual behavior

When creating a client scope on one node of the cluster, the user cache content for the realm where the client scope was created is completely cleared on the other nodes.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Deploy a Keycloak cluster of any version newer than 18.0.2 with user cache metrics enabled.

keycloak.conf
```
# cluster
cache=ispn
cache-config-file=cache-ispn.xml

# Observability
metrics-enabled=true
health-enabled=true

```

cache-ispn.xml (default, just enable statistics)
```
<infinispan
        xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
        xsi:schemaLocation="urn:infinispan:config:13.0 http://www.infinispan.org/schemas/infinispan-config-13.0.xsd"
        xmlns="urn:infinispan:config:13.0">
    <jgroups>
        <stack name="tcpping" extends="tcp">
            <TCP bind_port="7600" />
            <TCPPING
                initial_hosts="keycloak1[7600],keycloak2[7600],keycloak3[7600]"
                port_range="0"
                stack.combine="REPLACE"
                stack.position="MPING"
            />
        </stack>
    </jgroups>

    <cache-container name="keycloak" statistics="true">
        <transport lock-timeout="60000" stack="tcpping"/>
        <local-cache name="realms" simple-cache="true">
            <encoding>
                <key media-type="application/x-java-object"/>
                <value media-type="application/x-java-object"/>
            </encoding>
            <memory max-count="10000"/>
        </local-cache>
        <local-cache name="users" simple-cache="true" statistics="true">
            <encoding>
                <key media-type="application/x-java-object"/>
                <value media-type="application/x-java-object"/>
            </encoding>
            <memory max-count="10000"/>
        </local-cache>
...

```
2. Create a test realm and several users in it.
3. On all nodes of the cluster, perform a user search in the test realm through the admin web interface to populate the user cache.
4. Retrieve the metric from each cluster node showing the number of entries in the user cache.
```
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8082/auth/metrics | grep entries | grep users

vendor_cache_manager_keycloak_cache_users_statistics_approximate_entries{node="40f4b2ced666-5496"} 17.0
vendor_cache_manager_keycloak_cache_users_statistics_approximate_entries_in_memory{node="40f4b2ced666-5496"} 17.0
vendor_cache_manager_keycloak_cache_users_statistics_approximate_entries_unique{node="40f4b2ced666-5496"} 17.0
vendor_cache_manager_keycloak_cache_users_statistics_current_number_of_entries_in_memory{node="40f4b2ced666-5496"} 17.0
vendor_cache_manager_keycloak_cache_users_statistics_number_of_entries{node="40f4b2ced666-5496"} 17.0
```
5. On one of the nodes, create a client scope in the test realm.
6. Retrieve the metric from each cluster node again, showing the number of entries in the user cache. As a result, we will see that on the nodes where the client scope was not created, the entries in the user cache have been cleared.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Updating dynamically registered client's metadata drops `preferred_username` from ID token
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When one registers a client using a standard OIDC Dynamic Client Registration call, and then, sends an update request to the registered client's configuration endpoint, a strange side-effect happens: ID token loses `preferred_username`.
Not a problem if a client metadata is re-read from the registered client's  configuration endpoint.

### Version

25.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Authorization code flow involving the dynamically registered client produces correct ID tokens after the registered client's metadata have been updated.

### Actual behavior

ID token loses `preferred_username`

### How to Reproduce?

* Register a client with https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/securing_apps/index.html#openid-connect-dynamic-client-registration using one of the documented options (provide a `redirect_uris` array with a single value like `http://localhost:8080/protected`)
* Secure a Quarkus endpoint at `@Path("/protected")`, for example, a Quarkus endpoint with the registered client's id and secret, and `application-type=web-app` to enable an authorization code flow
* Have the endpoint code secured with `@Authenticated`
* Have `@Inject @IdToken JsonWebToken idToken;` and return `idToken.getName()` from a secured endpoint's `@GET` method
* Login, and confirm the user name is returned

Now, repeat, but after registering a client, follow immediately with an update to this client's configuration endpoint, for example, send the same metadata you got after the client has been registered.

Repeat other steps, ID token will have no `preferred_username`.

### Anything else?

I can prepare a test on my branch if the team decides to look at this issue

issue_title: MULTIVALUED_STRING_TYPE not displaying 1 value while more than 1 value is working fine
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Bug report from support customer.  

See https://issues.redhat.com/browse/SSOSUP-894

### Version

RHBK 24.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

IHAC who have created a custom user federation. In the custom user federation they are using property with type `MULTIVALUED_STRING_TYPE`.

When the value of this property is set to multiple values it is working as expected and the multiple values that are set is displayed in custom user federation configuration.
But when the value of this property is set to only 1 value, then that 1 value is not displayed in the configuration.
 

I am also able to replicate the issue in my local using one of the [rhbk quickstart ](https://github.com/redhat-developer/rhbk-quickstarts/tree/22.x/extension/user-storage-simple) . Attaching sample screenshot of the working and non working scenario. The value is returned by the server but it is not displayed in the browser.

See JIRA for screen shots.

### Actual behavior

Single value is shown.

### How to Reproduce?

Use https://github.com/redhat-developer/rhbk-quickstarts/tree/22.x/extension/user-storage-simple

issue_title: Not able to remove otp credential of user
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

account/api

### Describe the bug

Since version 24.0.3 onwards (24.0.2 works fine) users are not able to delete their otp credentials via the account api anymore.

The request looks like the following:
```
Request URL: http://localhost:8380/realms/x/account/credentials/87909a18-79b9-4f4f-b7d1-3f9fef1b526b
Request Method: DELETE
Status Code: 403 Forbidden
Content: {
    "error": "No LoA on the token",
    "error_description": "For more on this error consult the server log at the debug level."
}

```
And in the logs: `WARN  [org.keycloak.services.resources.account.AccountCredentialResource] (executor-thread-7) Not able to remove credential of user 'e@example.com' as no acr claim on the token`

Creating new credentials seems to be working without a problem.
GET-Response from http://localhost:8380/realms/x/account/credentials looks like the following:
```
[
    {
        "type": "password",
        "category": "basic-authentication",
        "displayName": "password-display-name",
        "helptext": "password-help-text",
        "iconCssClass": "kcAuthenticatorPasswordClass",
        "updateAction": "UPDATE_PASSWORD",
        "removeable": false,
        "userCredentialMetadatas": [
            {
                "credential": {
                    "id": "1986f2f1-213a-47db-be09-f79f24ecbd13",
                    "type": "password",
                    "createdDate": 1679072941676,
                    "credentialData": "{\"hashIterations\":210000,\"algorithm\":\"pbkdf2-sha512\",\"additionalParameters\":{}}"
                }
            }
        ]
    },
    {
        "type": "otp",
        "category": "two-factor",
        "displayName": "otp-display-name",
        "helptext": "otp-help-text",
        "iconCssClass": "kcAuthenticatorOTPClass",
        "createAction": "CONFIGURE_TOTP",
        "removeable": true,
        "userCredentialMetadatas": [
            {
                "credential": {
                    "id": "87909a18-79b9-4f4f-b7d1-3f9fef1b526b",
                    "type": "otp",
                    "createdDate": 1720439963400,
                    "credentialData": "{\"subType\":\"totp\",\"digits\":6,\"counter\":0,\"period\":30,\"algorithm\":\"HmacSHA1\"}"
                }
            }
        ]
    }
]
```
Do we need to configure now otp credentials differently? I did not find anything in the change notes for 24.0.3.

### Version

24.0.3+

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

User can delete their own otp credentials

### Actual behavior

user cannot

### How to Reproduce?

Setup otp authentication and then try to delete it via account api.

### Anything else?

Error messages originate from new code added with this commit: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/c427e65354a208cedbfb828e47e6a631f903b384

issue_title: inputOptionLabelsI18nPrefix is take into consideration only for login-ui
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

I want to translate a user attribute with annotation `inputType="select"`

Unfortunately if i set the annotation `inputOptionLabelsI18nPrefix` and a theme with custom messages, the messages take into account are only the files for login-ui.
Messages in admin-ui or account-ui does not work.

### Version

25.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I expect that if i customize a theme and a theme type with custom messages, using `inputOptionLabelsI18nPrefix` the labels are translated into the selected language.

### Actual behavior

See screenshot for Country field:

Login UI: [Correct]
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/48908421/297686e2-6313-4fdd-af28-6c53be35dc4a)

Account UI: [Wrong]
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/48908421/60f322ab-7c6c-4c24-a2de-0265b2d642a4)

issue_title: Review filtering of session returned from the sessions cache
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

There is some a dubious code path where we continue searching the different caches even if we know it doesn't match the predicate. 

See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/30859#discussion_r1668755619 for the original comment

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

* If an entry is returned from the cache, and it doesn't match the predicate, we shouldn't try any other store.

### Actual behavior

* If we don't have a match locally, we try to get it from the remote store

### How to Reproduce?

This is a code analysis.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Cannot set unmanagedAttributePolicy without profile attributes
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

With the new User Profile, we want to keep using the "old" attributes. For that we set the `unmanagedAttributePolicy` via the API to be `ENABLED`.

According to documentation, this should be possible by doing a PUT call to `/admin/realm/{realm}/users/profile` which requires a [UPConfig](https://www.keycloak.org/docs-api/24.0.1/rest-api/index.html#UPConfig), which has 3 **optional** parameters: `attributes`, `groups` & `unmanagedAttributePolicy`.
In practice, `groups` is optional, but `attributes` is **mandatory**.

### Version

25.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Setting `unmanagedAttributePolicy` shouldn't need the full attribute definition.

### Actual behavior

```
{"errorMessage":"[UserProfile configuration without 'attributes' section is not allowed]"}
```

### How to Reproduce?

```
curl --location --request PUT 'http://{keycloak.url}/admin/realms/{realm}/users/profile' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: ••••••' \
--data '{
    "unmanagedAttributePolicy": "ENABLED"
}'
```

### Anything else?

We have several ways to move forward:
1. mark `attributes` as mandatory in the documentation (does not require code changes). This is the "easy" solution, but feels a bit cumbersome to be forced to GET attributes, just to be able to PUT them back.
2. make `attributes` really **optional** (requires code changes).

Cannot set unmanagedAttributePolicy without profile attributes

Closes #31153

issue_title: Re-enabling a temporarily locked user (brute-force) deletes all user properties and attributes
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When re-enabling a due to brute-force temporarily locked user, all the user attributes and properties are being deleted, only the username remains.

### Version

25.x / nightly

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

User will be unlocked again, all attributes and properties are preserved.

### Actual behavior

User is unlocked again (expected), but all attributes and properties are deleted.

### How to Reproduce?

- create a fresh realm
- create a user with default properties and some additional attributes, assign a password
- enable brute-force-protection in the realm with temporary lockout
- try to authenticate with the created user, but with invalid password, until the max login failures are reached and the user is temporarily locked, e.g. with the account console
- in admin-ui, go to the user details, user is temporarily locked
- hit the switch to unlock the user -> two change requests are being sent to the server
- reload the browser to get a new, fresh set of user data, now the email, firstname and lastname on the first page are empty and all the other attributes are also gone!

### Anything else?

_No response_

Do not send attributes when unlocking the user

Closes #31165

issue_title: Single use tokens, like action tokens,  has a claim `expiration`
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When the return type of `getExp()` in org.keycloak.models.SingleUseObjectKeyModel was changed from an int to a Long, the old int behaviour was kept as a getter `getExpiration()` which has a default implementation to get the int value from `getExp()`.

This causes the JSON serialization to include the value from `getExpiration()` as a claim `expiration`. Since the `DefaultActionToken` extends `DefaultActionTokenKey` which implements `SingleUseObjectKeyModel`, a `expiration claim is now included in all action tokens.

### Version

25.0.1

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Action tokens should have a claim `exp` for expiration.

### Actual behavior

Action tokens has both  `exp` and `expiration` claims for expiration.

### How to Reproduce?

Use the forgotten password method and decode the reset password token. It has both `exp` and `expiration`.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Add @JsonIgnore to SingleUseObjectKeyModel's getExpiration()

Closes #31171

Signed-off-by: Johannes Knutsen <johannes@kodet.no>

issue_title: Realm export - duplicated elements in browser flow. Organization user must click login button multiple times.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

import-export

### Describe the bug

Bug happens during exporting to json previously imported realm from json.
Some elements in export file in authenticationExecutions of authenticationFlows  (**e.g browser flow**) are duplicated each time export is invoked.

For example if you develop app and exports realm to json than imports the realm during deploy, make changes and export again authentication flows elements are redundant. 

In context of organization when identity-first login (only email/username) is enabled duplicating elements makes user unable to login. 
User must press login button as many times as the count of realm exports bcs auth flow steps are redundant.



Left col:    base import file
Right col: exported realm that was previously imported
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/50982515/d623c792-433f-4ef6-affe-3fd22f0fb4ce)

Login button must be pressed as many times as export happend

Left col:    after import
Right col: exported realm that was previously imported
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/50982515/25ea0056-c8e2-4e8f-bd4d-6ba3cc2ba389)

Browser flow view:
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/50982515/ae9426d2-7622-4656-9e57-b1668fd25a0b)



### Version

Nightly (999.0.0-SNAPSHOT)

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Export feature not duplicates autheniticationExecutions steps in authentication flows in export.json file (e.g browser flow).

Organization user doesn't have to press login button as many times as export happend (in _identity-first login_ with organizations enabled)

### Actual behavior

Authentication flows elements/steps are duplicated each time export happens.

When organizations enabled and _identity-first login_ (only email/username) user press button one time to be prompt for password.

### How to Reproduce?

I got webapp that uses keycloak to secure:

1. Create realm

2. Enable organization + add some organization
3. Export realm to json file
4. Import realm with the file (I build new container and then import from admin UI console)

5. Repeat steps 2, 3 and 4 (simulates development process)
6. Add user with password, add organization (organizations aren't preserved while export)

7. Try to log user in to application
8. You have to press login as many times as count of the exports.


### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Avoid adding organization flows if they are already exist

Closes #31182

Signed-off-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>

issue_title: Offline tokens created in Keycloak 9 will not work on Keycloak 25
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Offline tokens created in Keycloak 9 will not work on Keycloak 25

Keycloak 9 refresh tokens did not include a `sid` claim, which is now used for the session identifier when looking up an offline session.

When an upgraded instance of Keycloak 25 receives this offline token, it will not be able to look up the session because the `sid` claim and associated session identifier is null.

```
Uncaught server error: java.lang.NullPointerException: Null keys are not supported!
	at java.base/java.util.Objects.requireNonNull(Objects.java:235)
	at org.infinispan.cache.impl.CacheImpl.assertKeyNotNull(CacheImpl.java:210)
	at org.infinispan.cache.impl.CacheImpl.get(CacheImpl.java:549)
	at org.infinispan.cache.impl.CacheImpl.get(CacheImpl.java:545)
	at org.infinispan.cache.impl.EncoderCache.get(EncoderCache.java:735)
	at org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.changes.InfinispanChangelogBasedTransaction.get(InfinispanChangelogBasedTransaction.java:140)
	at org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.InfinispanUserSessionProvider.getUserSessionEntity(InfinispanUserSessionProvider.java:347)
	at org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.InfinispanUserSessionProvider.getUserSession(InfinispanUserSessionProvider.java:275)
	at org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.InfinispanUserSessionProvider.getOfflineUserSession(InfinispanUserSessionProvider.java:817)
	at org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.InfinispanUserSessionProvider.getOfflineUserSession(InfinispanUserSessionProvider.java:94)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.UserSessionManager.findOfflineUserSession(UserSessionManager.java:75)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.TokenManager.validateToken(TokenManager.java:152)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.TokenManager.refreshAccessToken(TokenManager.java:402)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.grants.RefreshTokenGrantType.process(RefreshTokenGrantType.java:76)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.TokenEndpoint.processGrantRequest(TokenEndpoint.java:140)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.TokenEndpoint$quarkusrestinvoker$processGrantRequest_6408e15340992839b66447750c221d9aaa837bd7.invoke(Unknown Source)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.handlers.InvocationHandler.handle(InvocationHandler.java:29)
	at io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.runtime.QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.invokeHandler(QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:141)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.run(AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.java:147)
	at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder$14.runWith(VertxCoreRecorder.java:582)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2513)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1538)
	at org.jboss.threads.DelegatingRunnable.run(DelegatingRunnable.java:29)
	at org.jboss.threads.ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.run(ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.java:29)
	at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30)
	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:840)
```

### Version

25.0.1

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

This could go two ways - implement some backwards compatibility of some kind to allow these to continue to work OR it is called out explicitly in the upgrading guide so administrators can communicate to their users to get new tokens.

### Actual behavior

Offline sessions fail / administrators not aware upgrading to Keycloak 25 would break offline sessions created in a prior instance.

### How to Reproduce?

Deploy Keycloak 9, create a client that can receive offline token, save the offline token, upgrade the Keycloak instance to 25, attempt to use the offline token.

### Anything else?

sid claim added - https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/8c1ea60b048196fce5d2578eb31f690298752580

session_state removed - https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/b4f791b6321d95040a74eb2be93fd3f66f042114

Offline tokens created in Keycloak 14 or earlier will not work on Keycloak 25
closes #31224

issue_title: IdP redirect URL shows hostname_admin
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

We are using a dedicated `hostname-admin` URL.
When creating a new IdP (i,e SSO with clients' AD), we have to share the redirect URL. The URL contains the admin url and not the frontend url.

### Version

25.0.1

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Frontend/Backend URL should be used to expose URLs and not the Administration URL.

Keycloak `v25.0.0` works as expected!

### Actual behavior

The URL contains the admin url and not the frontend url.

### How to Reproduce?

#### Running keycloak `25.0.1`
```yaml
services:
  keycloak:
    image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:25.0.1
    command: ["start-dev"]
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      KEYCLOAK_ADMIN: admin
      KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD: password
      KC_HOSTNAME: http://localhost:8081
      KC_HOSTNAME_ADMIN: http://localhost:8082
    ports:
      - 8081:8080
      - 8082:8080
```
- browse to `http://localhost:8082` <- the admin console URL
- Go to `Identity providers` (bottom left main menu)
- create a new Identity Provider - doesn't matter which type (all have the same issue)
- Observe the Redirect URL: 
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/53181b47-7bc7-45b8-a8eb-49abbc00f1b8)
(shows the **admin** hostname)

#### Running keycloak `25.0.0`
```yaml
services:
  keycloak:
    image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:25.0.0
    command: ["start-dev"]
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      KEYCLOAK_ADMIN: admin
      KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD: password
      KC_HOSTNAME: http://localhost:8081
      KC_HOSTNAME_ADMIN: http://localhost:8082
    ports:
      - 8081:8080
      - 8082:8080
```
- browse to `http://localhost:8082` <- the admin console URL
- Go to `Identity providers` (bottom left main menu)
- create a new Identity Provider - doesn't matter which type (all have the same issue)
- Observe the Redirect URL: 
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b41761ef-41a0-44f8-87fe-6b83d1240c83)
(shows the **frontend** hostname)



### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Revoke access tokens for persistent user sessions
issue_body:
### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

When using persistent user sessions, user sessions will survive the restart of Keycloak. 
As revoked access tokens will be stored in-memory only, those entries will be gone, and the access tokens will be valid again. 

### Version

25.0.0

### Expected behavior

When using persistent sessions a revoked access token should still be revoked after a full Keycloak restart.

### Actual behavior

The access token is usable again after the Keycloak restart, as the user session is stored in the database, but the revoked access token is not.

Persisting revoked access tokens

Closes #31296

issue_title: dynamic MultiValuedListComponent default value not stringified
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

If you define a default value for a provider configuration property of type `MULTIVALUED_LIST_TYPE`, the default value is not stringified in the frontend `MultiValuedListComponent`, when used for an identity provider.

### Version

24.05

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Default value should be stringified, when property is defined for MultiValuedListComponent

### Actual behavior

Default value should be stringified, when property is defined for MultiValuedListComponent

### How to Reproduce?

Create a identity provider with its own configuration properties:
```java
ProviderConfigurationBuilder
            .create()
            .property()
            .name("custom multi select list")
            .type(ProviderConfigProperty.MULTIVALUED_LIST_TYPE)
            .options("item1", "item2", "item3")
            .defaultValue("item1")
            .add()
            .build();
```
Try to create a new instance of the IDP in the admin console. Default value is selected, but on save, the server will complain, because an array `["item1"]` is send, instead of the stringified version `"item1"`.

### Anything else?

Root cause is located in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/js/apps/admin-ui/src/components/dynamic/MultivaluedListComponent.tsx#L45

There is no check for `stringify`, so a possible solution could look like 
```ts
        defaultValue={
          defaultValue
            ? stringify
              ? toStringValue([defaultValue])
              : [defaultValue]
            : stringify
              ? ""
              : []
        }
```
commit_message:
keep default value a string when stringify (#31597)

issue_title: Misleading docs and functionality around cache-ispn.xml
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

The [docs](https://www.keycloak.org/server/caching) state: 

> Keycloak provides a cache configuration file with sensible defaults located at conf/cache-ispn.xml.

This could lead to the assumption that if this file is changed, the settings will come into effect (at least when `cache` is set to `ispn`). 

It also states:

>  You are free to change the number of owners accordingly to better fit into your availability requirements. To change the number of owners, open conf/cache-ispn.xml and change the value for owners=<value> for the distributed caches to your desired value.



### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When changing the file, those changes should come into effect once I edit the file (at least that's what the docs seem to tell me, and this is what I would assume when I see the file in the conf folder). 

### Actual behavior

The changes wouldn't come into effect unless I add the CLI parameter `--cache-config-file=cache-ispn.xml` as otherwise the file would be taken from the classpath which includes the shipped defaults.

### How to Reproduce?

This happend to me when I tried to enable the metrics in KC24 which need editing the `cache-ispn.xml`. A similar configuration mishap would happen if I change the number of cache entries. 

### Anything else?

While some of this could be fixed by the docs for KC24/KC25 where we don't want to change the functionality, keeping a file in the conf folder which contains the defaults but isn't used is part of the problem. 

Another idea: It might also be good in KC24/25 to print the file path where the ISPN file has been picked up from: 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/d2ae27a1e2ff0cf153f56e89c2dd3979eb6e7704/quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/KeycloakRecorder.java#L143

This might have been discussed in the past, so I'm eager to hear the cloud native team's ideas around that.
commit_message:
Read cache-ispn.xml from conf/ by default

Fixed #31492

Signed-off-by: Pedro Ruivo <pruivo@redhat.com>

Co-authored-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>
